---
name: Doctrine question
about: Ask why a design decision was made, or surface a doctrine ambiguity
title: "[Doctrine] "
labels: ["doctrine"]
assignees: ["nick-railsback"]
---

## The decision in question

<!-- Which call are you asking about? Quote the doctrine passage or name the chapter + section (e.g., "04-delivery.md §Per-verb permissions" or "the reviewer-in-the-loop rule"). -->

## What's unclear or worth pushing on

<!-- Are you asking why it's this way (background), how it interacts with another rule (consistency), whether an edge case breaks it (stress test), or whether the call should be revisited (challenge)? A sentence on which mode you're in helps shape the answer. -->

## What you tried first

<!-- Did you read the cited chapter, search prior issues/discussions, or work through the doctrine yourself? Pointing at what you already found helps avoid relitigating settled ground. -->

## Concrete situation (optional)

<!-- A real example that surfaced the question. "I was doing X and hit Y" beats "in general I wonder Z". Doctrine often turns on specifics. -->
